command: $ErrorActionPreference='Stop'; $pscp='pscp.exe'; $log="$env:ProgramData\NetAdmin\logs\T1597.log"; $host='sftp.flashpoint-intel.com'; $user=$env:FP_USER; $key=$env:FP_PPK; $hostkey=$env:FP_HOSTKEY; $remote='/feeds/closed_web/daily/closedweb-20250930.json.gz'; $dest='C:\Intel\Flashpoint\closedweb-20250930.json.gz'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $dest) -Force | Out-Null; try { & $pscp -scp -batch -q -C -P 22 -i $key -hostkey $hostkey "$user@$host:$remote" "$dest"; if($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0){ throw "pscp exit code $LASTEXITCODE" }; $hash=(Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 -Path $dest).Hash; $status=[pscustomobject]@{ technique='T1597'; source=$host; remote_path=$remote; local_path=$dest; sha256=$hash; success=$true; ts=(Get-Date).ToString('o') } } catch { $status=[pscustomobject]@{ technique='T1597'; source=$host; remote_path=$remote; local_path=$dest; success=$false; error=$_.Exception.Message; ts=(Get-Date).ToString('o') } } finally { $json=$status|ConvertTo-Json -Compress; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $log) -Force | Out-Null; Add-Content -Path $log -Value $json; Write-Output $json }
explanation: Collects a subscription-only closed-web intelligence feed from Flashpoint using SCP with host key pinning, then records a JSON audit entry.
rationale_steps: Connect to a paid, closed-source intelligence provider with pinned SSH host key.
Transfer compressed JSON data using quiet, non-interactive mode for operational security.
Compute SHA-256 and log a structured acquisition record.
Limits scope to closed-source collection, avoiding other techniques.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: PowerShell is well-formed with balanced braces/quotes, valid parameter usage, proper try/catch/finally structure, and correct invocation of pscp.exe.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It fetches a subscription-only feed from a closed-source provider using authenticated file transfer with host key pinning and records a JSON audit, directly aligning with T1597.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced quotes, braces, and parentheses, proper semicolon separators, valid try/catch/finally blocks, and correct flag syntax for pscp.exe.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately models T1597 by downloading a subscription-only closed-web intelligence feed from Flashpoint via SCP with credential and host-key pinning, logging a structured audit entry, though using pscp.exe (PuTTY SCP) rather than native PowerShell SFTP is a minor realism gap, and the technique typically describes adversaries querying/purchasing data rather than scripted automated retrieval from a legitimate subscription service.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell script uses correct syntax, proper variable interpolation, valid try-catch-finally blocks, and appropriate command separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately emulates the acquisition of closed-source intelligence (T1597) by downloading a non-public feed from a known threat-intelligence provider (Flashpoint) using standard protocols and logging the outcome.
